what_it_does: This file is the package-level docstring for company/introspection. It describes the package as a platform-agnostic Mirror-Registry System engine plus instance adapters, outlines the four-verb circuit DISCOVER -> CLASSIFY -> PROJECT -> REFRESH operating over Platform and Capability Registries, notes that platform names do not live in engine/rules/adapters code, and states that the __init__ remains side-effect-free by not auto-importing submodules.
observations: The file contains only a module docstring; no executable code or symbols.
It names a four-verb circuit: DISCOVER, CLASSIFY, PROJECT, REFRESH.
It distinguishes a Platform Registry and a Capability Registry.
It asserts that no platform names appear in engine/rules/adapters; Claude Code is a single data row in platforms/claude_code.py.
It references AGENTS.md as the folder constitution and several F-FIX design items (F-FIX-1, F-FIX-2, F-FIX-15, F-FIX-10).
It explicitly avoids import-time side effects by not importing submodules.
standouts: The __init__ is intentionally side-effect-free; callers must import submodules explicitly.
Platform-specific naming is pushed out of the engine into a separate platforms data file.
The docstring acts as a manifest/constitution pointer rather than wiring code.
conventions: Cross-referencing design documents and fix IDs in code comments.
Separation of engine/rules/adapters from platform-specific data rows.
Explicit statement of import discipline to avoid side effects.
notes: This file is a pure package manifest: it tells readers what introspection is meant to be and how to import it, but contains no runnable logic.
questions: What concrete operations do DISCOVER, CLASSIFY, PROJECT, and REFRESH perform?
What schemas populate the Platform Registry and Capability Registry?
What do the F-FIX-1, F-FIX-2, F-FIX-15, and F-FIX-10 design rules specify?
What is the NEW-pattern singleton and the transport-derivation contract?
How is the store write convention enforced?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the introspection package and keep imports side-effect-free; the actual content is only a docstring doing exactly that.
apparent_themes: Separation of engine logic from instance data
intention_signals: The file signals an attempt to build a reusable, rule-governed introspection engine whose boundaries are enforced by documentation and import discipline, with platform specifics kept outside the core.
novelty: Using __init__.py primarily as a constitutional manifest that points to AGENTS.md and design-rule IDs, while deliberately refusing to wire submodules at import time.
contribution: It contributes a clear, self-describing package boundary that records scope, design-rule lineage, and import policy, which a fused system can rely on to locate engine logic and respect side-effect-free loading.
summary_for_embedding: Package-level docstring for company/introspection that defines a side-effect-free, platform-agnostic Mirror-Registry engine operating a four-verb circuit over Platform and Capability Registries.
intention_for_embedding: To frame the introspection package as a documented, import-safe entry point whose engine and adapter modules are loaded explicitly by callers.
